extra.reasoning: The input variable `dizziness` does not appear in any rule (r1-r8) nor in the default. All eight rules trigger only on the other inputs: fever (r1), syncope (r2), sweating (r3), vision_loss (r4), leg_swelling (r5), focal_weakness (r6), abdominal_pain (r7), vomiting (r8). The default sets acuity := routine when no rule fires. Because the monotonicity property holds the other eight inputs fixed and only toggles dizziness from false to true, the exact same set of rules matches in both cases, and conflict resolution (priority then order) yields an identical winning assignment. Therefore acuity is exactly equal before and after setting dizziness true — it can never be lowered. The property "setting dizziness true never lowers acuity" is satisfied for the entire input space (the stronger fact, equality, holds). No counterexample exists.
